title: Intensely Creamy Australian Cheese Flatbread
description: A savory and creamy Australian dish featuring kaffir lime leaf, perfect for kids and adults alike, made with long fermentation dough and ovo-vegetarian friendly ingredients.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, sugar, and yeast, then gradually add olive oil and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic, then place in a greased bowl, cover, and let ferment for 24 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the fermented dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Transfer the dough to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Sprinkle shredded cheese evenly over the dough, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
In a small bowl, whisk together eggs and cream, then pour the mixture over the cheese.
Add kaffir lime leaves on top of the egg mixture.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the eggs are set.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
